title: How Did Humans Learn How the Body and Mind Work?
description: An interactive branching history of physiology, psychology, and neuroscience from ancient medicine to modern science.

theme.motif: laboratories, anatomy sketches, brain maps, and scientific instruments

narrator.name: Dr. Sophia
narrator.intro: I am Dr. Sophia. Read the clue, guess the next discovery, then reveal how body and mind science grew.
wrongLines: Good hypothesis, but the evidence points elsewhere.
Not quite — science took another path.

missions.title: The Search Begins
missions.description: Discover how ancient thinkers tried to explain body and mind.

missions.title: Evidence Replaces Authority
missions.description: See observation and experiments transform medicine.

missions.title: Books That Changed Everything
missions.description: Follow ideas that survived through texts and teaching.

missions.title: The Brain Reveals Its Secrets
missions.description: Trace discoveries that turned mind study into neuroscience.

missions.title: The Next Frontier
missions.description: Explore possible futures of physiology, psychology, and medicine.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ro.title: How Did Humans Learn How the Body and Mind Work?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ro.summary: Today we use MRI scanners, neuroscience, psychology, and physiology. But for most of history people did not know how blood moved, how nerves worked, or where thoughts came from. This is the story of humanity's attempt to understand itself.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

prediction.question: Who do you think had the greatest influence on medicine for the next 1,500 years?

Correct. "Galen" is the strongest answer for this historical moment.
Good guess, but "Nobody" is not the strongest answer here.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hi.title: Hippocrates
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hi.summary: Around 400 BCE, Hippocrates argued that disease had natural causes rather than supernatural ones. This idea helped transform medicine from ritual into investigation.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hi.title: The Hippocratic Corpus
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hi.summary: A collection of medical writings associated with Hippocrates. It became one of the foundational texts of Western medicine.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hu.title: The Four Humors
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hu.summary: Health was believed to depend on balancing blood, phlegm, yellow bile, and black bile. Though incorrect, this theory influenced medicine for centuries.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ar.title: Aristotle
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ar.summary: Aristotle studied animals extensively and attempted to explain life through observation. He believed the heart, not the brain, was the center of thought.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.title: Comparative Anatomy
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.summary: By comparing different animals, Aristotle created one of the earliest systematic biological research programs.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: Alexandria Anatomists
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: In ancient Alexandria, scholars gained opportunities to study human anatomy more directly than many earlier physicians.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::he.title: Herophilus
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::he.summary: Herophilus distinguished nerves from blood vessels and argued that the brain played a major role in intelligence.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::er.title: Erasistratus
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::er.summary: Erasistratus proposed mechanical explanations for bodily processes and contributed early ideas about circulation.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ga.title: Galen
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ga.summary: Galen became the most influential medical authority in history. His writings dominated medicine for over a millennium.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

prediction.question: What happens when one scientist becomes the accepted authority for 1,500 years?

choices.feedback: Good guess, but "Rapid progress" is not the strongest answer here.
Correct. "Progress slows" is the strongest answer for this historical moment.

Good guess, but "Knowledge disappears" is not the strongest answer here.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ga.title: The Books of Galen
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ga.summary: Works such as 'On the Usefulness of the Parts of the Body' became standard medical references throughout Europe and the Middle East.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::is.title: Islamic Golden Age
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::is.summary: Scholars preserved, translated, criticized, and expanded classical medical knowledge.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: Al-Razi
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: Al-Razi emphasized observation and clinical medicine, helping move medical practice toward evidence.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::av.title: Avicenna
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::av.summary: Avicenna's Canon of Medicine became one of the most influential medical textbooks ever written.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ca.title: Canon of Medicine
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ca.summary: For centuries, students across multiple civilizations learned medicine from this book.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::re.title: The Renaissance
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::re.summary: European scholars increasingly questioned ancient authorities and returned to direct observation.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ve.title: Andreas Vesalius
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ve.summary: Vesalius carefully studied anatomy and found numerous errors in Galen's descriptions.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::de.title: De Humani Corporis Fabrica
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::de.summary: Published in 1543, this landmark anatomy book challenged centuries of accepted medical doctrine.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ha.title: William Harvey
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ha.summary: Harvey demonstrated that blood circulates continuously through the body, overturning major elements of Galenic physiology.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::de.title: De Motu Cordis
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::de.summary: Harvey's book used observation and measurement to explain circulation.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: What If Harvey Had Been Ignored?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: If physicians had rejected Harvey's evidence, modern physiology might have been delayed by generations.

powerUp.title: Counterfactual Compass
powerUp.text: You tested a what-if path. Ask what changed, what stayed the same, and what evidence still matters.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ha.title: Albrecht von Haller
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ha.summary: Often called the father of modern physiology, Haller investigated how muscles and nerves function.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::el.title: Elements of Physiology
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::el.summary: Haller's monumental physiology text synthesized knowledge of bodily functions.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::mu.title: Johannes Müller
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::mu.summary: Müller connected anatomy, physiology, and sensory experience, influencing both physiology and psychology.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::he.title: Hermann von Helmholtz
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::he.summary: Helmholtz measured nerve conduction speed, proving that nervous processes were measurable physical events.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::be.title: Claude Bernard
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::be.summary: Bernard established experimental physiology and introduced the idea of the body's internal environment.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ex.title: Introduction to Experimental Medicine
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ex.summary: Bernard argued that controlled experimentation should guide medical science.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::wu.title: Wilhelm Wundt
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::wu.summary: In 1879, Wundt established a psychology laboratory, helping create psychology as an independent scientific discipline.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fi.title: The First Psychology Laboratory
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fi.summary: Psychology began moving from philosophy toward experimental science.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ja.title: William James
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ja.summary: James explored consciousness, attention, habits, and adaptation.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::pr.title: Principles of Psychology
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::pr.summary: One of the most influential psychology books ever written.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fr.title: Sigmund Freud
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fr.summary: Freud proposed that unconscious processes shape behavior and personality.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::pa.title: Ivan Pavlov
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::pa.summary: While studying digestion, Pavlov discovered that animals could learn associations between stimuli. His work connected physiology and psychology more directly than ever before.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.title: Conditioned Reflexes
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.summary: A neutral stimulus could become associated with a biological response through learning.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::be.title: Behaviorism
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::be.summary: Behaviorists argued that psychology should focus on observable behavior rather than invisible mental processes.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::wa.title: John B. Watson
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::wa.summary: Watson launched behaviorism as a major movement, arguing that behavior could be explained scientifically through learning.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::li.summary: Watson attempted to demonstrate how fears could be learned through conditioning.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::sk.title: B. F. Skinner
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::sk.summary: Skinner developed operant conditioning, explaining behavior through reinforcement and consequences.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::op.summary: Behavior can increase or decrease depending on rewards and punishments.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ge.summary: Gestalt psychologists argued that perception cannot be understood by studying isolated parts alone.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar
powerUp.text: You found a historically grounded step. Watch how observation, texts, and experiments build knowledge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hu.title: Humanistic Psychology
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hu.summary: Humanistic psychologists emphasized meaning, growth, creativity, and personal experience.

powerUp.title: Evidence Radar

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.title: The Cognitive Revolution
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.summary: Psychologists returned to studying memory, attention, language, and thinking using scientific methods.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::co.summary: Psychology, neuroscience, linguistics, computer science, and philosophy began working together.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fi.title: The Mind and Body Reunited
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fi.summary: Ancient physicians asked where thoughts came from and how the body worked. Today, physiology, psychology, and neuroscience form a connected scientific enterprise. The journey that began with Hippocrates and Aristotle continues as humanity explores consciousness, intelligence, disease, and life itself.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hm.title: Patient H.M.
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::hm.summary: The study of a patient with severe memory impairment revealed that memory consists of multiple systems rather than a single faculty.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::ph.summary: A brain injury dramatically altered Gage's personality, suggesting specific brain regions contribute to behavior.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::br.summary: Broca linked speech production to a specific region of the brain.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: What If Microscopes Were Never Invented?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: Cells, neurons, bacteria, and many physiological processes would have remained hidden.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: What If Cajal Never Studied Neurons?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: The neuron doctrine may have taken decades longer to emerge.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: What If Homeostasis Was Never Discovered?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: Many diseases would remain poorly understood because scientists would lack a framework for internal regulation.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: What If Pavlov Never Found Conditioned Reflexes?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: Behaviorism may have developed very differently.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.title: What If AI Becomes Better Than Human Doctors?
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::al.summary: Future medicine could shift toward human-AI partnerships where diagnosis and treatment planning are largely automated.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fu.title: The Ethics of Understanding Ourselves
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fu.summary: As neuroscience and AI advance, society must decide how much control humans should have over memory, behavior, intelligence, and biology.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fu.title: The Mystery of Consciousness
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::fu.summary: Despite thousands of years of progress, scientists still debate how subjective experience emerges from physical processes.

history-of-physiology-and-psychology::gr.title: From Hippocrates to the Future
history-of-physiology-and-psychology::gr.summary: The story began with physicians trying to explain disease and philosophers wondering about thought. Over 2,400 years, physiology explained bodily functions, psychology explored behavior and cognition, and neuroscience connected mind to brain. The journey is unfinished. Future generations will continue asking the same question: how do bodies create minds, and how can understanding ourselves improve human life?
